subject: computer-science
description: Internet Email Communications MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: A programme used to view World Wide Web (WWW) pages, such as Chrome or Internet Explorer, is called a:
mcqs.explanation: A programme used to view WWW pages is called a browser — examples include Google Chrome, Mozilla Firefox, Internet Explorer and Yahoo.

mcqs.q: Which organisation standardises the World Wide Web (WWW)?
mcqs.explanation: The World Wide Web (WWW) is standardised by W3C (World Wide Web Consortium).

mcqs.q: WAP stands for:
mcqs.explanation: WAP stands for Wireless Application Protocol — a technical standard for accessing information over a mobile wireless network.

mcqs.q: HTML is best described as:
options: The standard markup language for documents displayed in a web browser
mcqs.explanation: HTML (Hypertext Markup Language) is the standard markup language for documents designed to be displayed in a web browser.

mcqs.q: Which of the following was a common high-end Internet service in the 1990s and early 2000s?
mcqs.explanation: ISDN (Integrated Services Digital Network) was a common high-end Internet service in the 1990s and early 2000s.

mcqs.q: A computer on the internet is uniquely identified by its:
mcqs.explanation: A computer on the internet is identified by its IP (Internet Protocol) address.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is used to protect a network server from damage by unauthorised users?
mcqs.explanation: A firewall is used to protect a network server from damage by those who log into the computer without authorisation.

mcqs.q: File Explorer in Windows was previously known as:
mcqs.explanation: File Explorer was previously known as Windows Explorer — it is a file manager application.

mcqs.q: In which year was Facebook launched?
mcqs.explanation: Facebook, one of the most popular social networking sites, was launched in 2004.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is NOT a search engine?
mcqs.explanation: Facebook.com is a social networking site — NOT a search engine.

mcqs.q: Which is considered the oldest internet search engine?
mcqs.explanation: Yahoo is considered the oldest internet search engine.

mcqs.q: Which is the largest (biggest) search engine on the internet?
mcqs.explanation: Google is the largest (biggest) search engine on the internet.

mcqs.q: Wikipedia is best described as:
mcqs.explanation: Wikipedia is an online encyclopedia — collaboratively edited by volunteers.

mcqs.q: Gmail is an example of:
mcqs.explanation: Gmail is a famous email programme provided by Google.

mcqs.q: Browser history keeps a list of:
options: Web pages visited in the current session
mcqs.explanation: Browser history keeps a list of web pages you have visited in the current session.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is a correctly formed email address?
mcqs.explanation: ppsc@punjab.gov.pk is a correctly formed email address — it follows the standard local-part@domain format.

mcqs.q: www.yahoo.com is an example of a:
mcqs.explanation: www.yahoo.com is a URL (Uniform Resource Locator) — the address used to access a web page.

mcqs.q: Electronic mail works on which principle?
mcqs.explanation: Electronic mail works on the client/server principle — a client program enables the user to interact with a server to access information and services.

mcqs.q: To read and send mail, users need to access:
options: The computer where their mail resides (the server)
mcqs.explanation: To read and send mail, users need to access the computer where their mail resides — the mail server.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is the function of an email CLIENT application?
options: Letting users read, reply, forward, compose and send messages
mcqs.explanation: The email client application is the interface that lets a user read, reply to, forward, compose and send new messages.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is an example of an e-mail client programme?
mcqs.explanation: Qualcomm Eudora, pine, elm and Lotus cc:Mail are examples of e-mail client programmes.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is NOT typically considered an e-mail client programme?
mcqs.explanation: Adobe Flash is multimedia software, NOT an e-mail client. pine, elm and Lotus cc:Mail are all e-mail clients.

mcqs.q: Compared to telephone or fax, email is:
options: The cheapest and most convenient form of communication
mcqs.explanation: Email messages are the cheapest and most convenient form of communication compared to telephone or fax.

mcqs.q: Email and websites are transmitted through:
mcqs.explanation: Email and websites are transmitted through Intranet, Internet and Extranet.

mcqs.q: Which of the following social media apps does NOT provide an email feature?
mcqs.explanation: WhatsApp is a social media / messaging app — it does NOT provide an email feature. Gmail, Outlook and Yahoo Mail are email services.

mcqs.q: In an email, the part to the LEFT of the @ symbol is called the:
mcqs.explanation: In an email address, the part to the left of the @ symbol is the local part (or username); the part to the right is the domain.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is the most likely outcome of using email instead of fax/phone?
mcqs.explanation: Email is the cheapest and most convenient form of communication — it is faster, lower cost and avoids paperwork compared to fax or telephone.

mcqs.q: What is spear phishing?
options: An email/electronic communications scam targeted at a specific individual, organisation or business
mcqs.explanation: Spear phishing is an email or electronic communications scam targeted towards a specific individual, organisation or business — unlike generic phishing which is mass-mailed.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is the process of taking plain text and scrambling it into an unreadable format?
mcqs.explanation: Encryption is the process of taking plain text — like a text message or email — and scrambling it into an unreadable format that only authorised parties can decrypt.

mcqs.q: What is a cookie in the context of web browsing?
options: A small text file stored on the hard drive of a user's terminal when he/she visits a website
mcqs.explanation: A cookie is a small text file stored on the hard drive of a user's terminal when he/she visits a website — used for session management, preferences, etc.

mcqs.q: What is hacking?
options: The process of writing legitimate code
Unauthorised and illegal accessing of computer programmes, often with criminal intent
mcqs.explanation: Hacking is the term used to denote the unauthorised and illegal accessing of computer programmes, often with criminal intent.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is a SECURITY threat to email users?
mcqs.explanation: Spear phishing is a major security threat to email users — it targets specific individuals or organisations to steal information or money.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is true about cookies?
options: They are small text files saved by a website on the user's terminal
mcqs.explanation: Cookies are small text files stored on a user's terminal by a website to remember preferences, session data and login states.

mcqs.q: What does the term Internet refer to?
options: A single network in one office
mcqs.explanation: Internet refers to a global information system — the worldwide network of interconnected networks.

mcqs.q: An Intranet is best described as:
options: A public web open to all
A company's internal web that uses TCP/IP, HTTP and other internet protocols
mcqs.explanation: An Intranet is a company's internal web that uses TCP/IP, HTTP and other internet protocols — but is restricted to that organisation.

mcqs.q: What is an Extranet?
options: A web within a web — a collaborative network using internet protocols for business purposes
mcqs.explanation: Extranet is described as a 'web within a web' — a collaborative network that uses internet protocols for business purposes (often connecting partners and suppliers).

mcqs.q: Which of the following uses TCP/IP and HTTP but is restricted to one company?
mcqs.explanation: An Intranet uses TCP/IP, HTTP and other internet protocols but is restricted to a single company's internal users.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is the BROADEST in scope?
mcqs.explanation: The Internet is the broadest in scope — it is a global information system. Extranet and Intranet are subsets of the same protocols restricted in access.

mcqs.q: In what year did Lawrence Roberts and Thomas Merrill connect computers in Massachusetts and California, creating the first WAN?
mcqs.explanation: In 1965, Lawrence Roberts and Thomas Merrill communicated between computers in Massachusetts and California — creating the first WAN and laying the groundwork for the internet.

mcqs.q: ARPANet — the first version of the internet — was created in which year?
mcqs.explanation: ARPANet, the first version of the internet, was created in 1969 and used to link computers at UCLA and Stanford.

mcqs.q: In 1969, ARPANet linked computers at:
mcqs.explanation: In 1969, ARPANet was used to link computers at UCLA and Stanford.

mcqs.q: Who published the 1974 paper that laid the groundwork for TCP/IP?
mcqs.explanation: In 1974, Robert Kahn and Vinton Cerf published 'A Protocol for Packet Network Communication', laying the groundwork for TCP/IP. The word 'internet' was first used in this paper.

mcqs.q: In which year was the word 'internet' first used?
mcqs.explanation: The word 'internet' was first used in 1974 in the TCP/IP paper by Kahn and Cerf.

mcqs.q: NSFNET — the backbone of the modern internet — was founded in:
mcqs.explanation: NSFNET was founded in 1986, providing the investment and backbone needed to create the internet as we know it today.

mcqs.q: Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web and the first web browser in:
mcqs.explanation: In 1989, Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web and the first web browser — opening the door for the internet to go mainstream.

mcqs.q: What was the name of the first commercial ISP launched in 1989?
mcqs.explanation: In 1989, the first commercial ISP — 'The World' — launched.

mcqs.q: Mosaic — the first web browser available to the public — launched in:
mcqs.explanation: Mosaic, the first web browser available to the public, launched in 1993.

mcqs.q: Netscape Navigator was released in stores in:
mcqs.explanation: Netscape Navigator was released in stores in 1994 — quickly becoming the dominant web browser of the mid-1990s.

mcqs.q: By the year 2000, approximately how many people were online worldwide?
mcqs.explanation: By 2000, approximately 300 million people around the world were officially online.

mcqs.q: In which year was the iPhone released, sparking the mobile internet revolution?
mcqs.explanation: The iPhone was released in 2007, giving rise to the mobile internet revolution.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is NOT typically listed as a use of the Internet?
mcqs.explanation: Microwave cooking is unrelated to the Internet. Social Networking, E-Commerce, Online Banking, Education, Research and Email are all standard uses of the Internet.

mcqs.q: Online shopping is also known as:
mcqs.explanation: Online shopping is also known as E-Commerce — the buying and selling of goods and services over the internet.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is the most common use of the Internet for ordinary users?
options: Social networking, email, browsing and online services
mcqs.explanation: For ordinary users, the most common Internet uses are social networking, email, web browsing, online banking, e-commerce, education and research.

mcqs.q: Cashless transactions, online banking and e-commerce are all categorised as:
mcqs.explanation: Cashless transactions, online banking and e-commerce are all standard uses of the Internet.

mcqs.q: Which of the following is NOT a typical use of the Internet?
mcqs.explanation: Defragmenting a hard disk is a local OS maintenance task — NOT an internet use. File Transfer, Real-time Updates and Job Search are typical internet uses.
